mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, leading to negative corporate earnings revisions and lower equity prices.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns, with a signal Sharpe ratio exceeding 0.1 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields statistically significant negative forward returns during tightening periods with a p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 3.5 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5 percent are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation shocks rather than cyclical weakness, supporting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS and UNRATE co-movement fails to predict returns, with an information coefficient below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, with a t-test p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below negative 0.5 percent signals manufacturing contraction, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines greater than 0.5 percent are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances.
